questions.prompt: Which Italian city-state, dominated by the Medici family, became the leading center of the Italian Renaissance during the 15th century?
questions.correctText: Florence

questions.prompt: Petrarch is most associated with which intellectual movement that emphasized the study of classical Greek and Roman texts?
questions.correctText: Humanism

questions.prompt: Machiavelli's 1513 work 'The Prince' argued that political rulers should primarily be guided by which principle?
questions.correctText: Practical effectiveness over moral ideals

questions.prompt: Civic humanism, as developed in Florence, especially emphasized which of the following?
questions.correctText: Active participation in republican government

questions.prompt: Which Northern Humanist authored 'In Praise of Folly' and prepared a critical Greek edition of the New Testament?
questions.correctText: Desiderius Erasmus

questions.prompt: Thomas More's 1516 work 'Utopia' is best understood as a critique of which contemporary society?
questions.correctText: Henry VIII's England

questions.prompt: Johannes Gutenberg's mid-15th-century innovation had its most transformative effect by enabling which of the following?
choices: Mass production and circulation of texts
questions.correctText: Mass production and circulation of texts

questions.prompt: Martin Luther's 95 Theses of 1517 directly attacked which Catholic practice?
questions.correctText: Sale of indulgences

questions.prompt: Luther's doctrine of justification by faith alone (sola fide) rejected which of the following?
choices: Salvation through good works and sacraments
questions.correctText: Salvation through good works and sacraments

questions.prompt: John Calvin's theology, centered in Geneva, is most distinctively associated with which doctrine?
questions.correctText: Predestination

questions.prompt: The Anabaptists were considered radical reformers primarily because they advocated which practice?
choices: Adult baptism upon profession of faith
questions.correctText: Adult baptism upon profession of faith

questions.prompt: Henry VIII broke with Rome and was declared Supreme Head of the Church of England by which act of Parliament?
questions.correctText: The Act of Supremacy 1534

questions.prompt: The Council of Trent (1545-1563) responded to the Protestant Reformation by doing which of the following?
choices: Endorsing Luther's doctrine of sola scriptura
Reaffirming Catholic doctrine and reforming clerical abuses
Granting laypeople the right to interpret scripture freely
Abolishing the sale of indulgences entirely
questions.correctText: Reaffirming Catholic doctrine and reforming clerical abuses

questions.prompt: The Society of Jesus, founded by Ignatius Loyola in 1540, was most influential through which activity?
questions.correctText: Education and overseas missionary work

questions.prompt: The Index of Forbidden Books, established by the Catholic Church in 1559, was primarily intended to do which of the following?
choices: Catalogue all books published in Europe
questions.correctText: Prevent Protestant ideas from spreading

questions.prompt: The Edict of Nantes (1598), issued by Henry IV, granted French Huguenots which of the following?
choices: Limited religious toleration and fortified towns
The right to control the monarchy
questions.correctText: Limited religious toleration and fortified towns

questions.prompt: The Peace of Westphalia (1648) is widely credited with establishing which foundational principle of international politics?
questions.correctText: State sovereignty and non-interference

questions.prompt: The Thirty Years' War (1618-1648) began primarily as a conflict in which region?
questions.correctText: Bohemia

questions.prompt: European witch hunts between 1500 and 1700 disproportionately targeted which group?
questions.correctText: Older, often poor, women

questions.prompt: Vasco da Gama's 1498 voyage was significant because it accomplished which feat?
choices: Reaching India by sailing around Africa
questions.correctText: Reaching India by sailing around Africa

questions.prompt: The Treaty of Tordesillas (1494) divided non-European lands between which two powers?
questions.correctText: Spain and Portugal

questions.prompt: The Columbian Exchange refers to which historical phenomenon?
choices: Transatlantic exchange of plants, animals, people, and diseases
Migration of Italian merchants to Iberia
questions.correctText: Transatlantic exchange of plants, animals, people, and diseases

questions.prompt: The dramatic 16th-century European 'price revolution' is most directly attributed to which factor?
choices: An influx of New World silver
questions.correctText: An influx of New World silver

questions.prompt: Joint-stock companies such as the Dutch and English East India Companies are best described as which of the following?
choices: State monopolies wholly owned by monarchs
Private firms that pooled investor capital and shared risk
questions.correctText: Private firms that pooled investor capital and shared risk

questions.prompt: Mercantilism, the dominant economic theory of 17th-century European states, emphasized which goal?
choices: Achieving a favorable balance of trade by maximizing exports
questions.correctText: Achieving a favorable balance of trade by maximizing exports

questions.prompt: By the mid-17th century, the Dutch Republic achieved commercial primacy in Europe largely through which of the following?
choices: Innovations in shipbuilding, finance, and Baltic trade
questions.correctText: Innovations in shipbuilding, finance, and Baltic trade

questions.prompt: Louis XIV's construction of the Palace of Versailles served which political purpose?
choices: To centralize power by tying nobles to the court
To provide refuge during foreign invasions
questions.correctText: To centralize power by tying nobles to the court

questions.prompt: Jean-Baptiste Colbert pursued which economic strategy on behalf of Louis XIV?
choices: Strict mercantilism through subsidies, tariffs, and monopolies
Abolition of internal tolls and guilds
questions.correctText: Strict mercantilism through subsidies, tariffs, and monopolies

questions.prompt: Louis XIV's revocation of the Edict of Nantes in 1685 had which major consequence?
choices: Spread of Calvinism throughout Catholic France
Exile of skilled Huguenots, weakening France's economy
Establishment of religious freedom in the colonies
questions.correctText: Exile of skilled Huguenots, weakening France's economy

questions.prompt: Maria Theresa of Austria is best remembered for which achievement?
choices: Defending Habsburg lands during the War of Austrian Succession and modernizing administration
questions.correctText: Defending Habsburg lands during the War of Austrian Succession and modernizing administration

questions.prompt: Joseph II of Austria is often considered the archetypal enlightened despot because he did which of the following?
choices: Granted religious toleration and tried to abolish serfdom
questions.correctText: Granted religious toleration and tried to abolish serfdom

questions.prompt: Frederick William, the Great Elector of Brandenburg-Prussia, laid the foundation of Prussian power chiefly by doing which of the following?
choices: Building a strong standing army and bureaucracy
Opening the kingdom to free trade
questions.correctText: Building a strong standing army and bureaucracy

questions.prompt: Frederick II 'the Great' of Prussia is best remembered for which of the following?
choices: Military victories and enlightened administrative reforms
Conquering France during the Seven Years' War
questions.correctText: Military victories and enlightened administrative reforms

questions.prompt: Peter the Great of Russia is most associated with which of the following?
choices: Forcing the westernization and modernization of Russia
questions.correctText: Forcing the westernization and modernization of Russia

questions.prompt: Catherine the Great of Russia is often described as an enlightened despot whose reforms were undercut by which event?
choices: Pugachev's rebellion and the French Revolution
questions.correctText: Pugachev's rebellion and the French Revolution

questions.prompt: The English Civil War (1642-1649) culminated in which dramatic outcome?
choices: Restoration of Charles I to absolute power
Execution of Charles I and establishment of a Commonwealth
Catholic victory and re-conversion of England
questions.correctText: Execution of Charles I and establishment of a Commonwealth

questions.prompt: Oliver Cromwell's Protectorate is best characterized as which of the following?
choices: A military-religious dictatorship by the Lord Protector
A democratic republic with universal suffrage
questions.correctText: A military-religious dictatorship by the Lord Protector

questions.prompt: The Glorious Revolution of 1688 resulted in which constitutional outcome?
choices: Establishment of absolute monarchy in England
Replacement of James II by William and Mary and the English Bill of Rights
Union of England and Scotland under a republic
questions.correctText: Replacement of James II by William and Mary and the English Bill of Rights

questions.prompt: The English Bill of Rights (1689) most directly limited which of the following?
choices: The powers of the monarch in favor of Parliament
questions.correctText: The powers of the monarch in favor of Parliament

questions.prompt: Copernicus' 'On the Revolutions of the Heavenly Spheres' (1543) is best known for proposing which of the following?
choices: The heliocentric model of the universe
questions.correctText: The heliocentric model of the universe

questions.prompt: Galileo's 1633 trial before the Roman Inquisition was primarily over his defense of which scientific position?
questions.correctText: The Copernican heliocentric model

questions.prompt: Johannes Kepler's contribution to the Scientific Revolution was best captured in his discovery that planets travel in what kind of orbit?
questions.correctText: Elliptical orbits

questions.prompt: Isaac Newton's 1687 'Principia Mathematica' is most famous for articulating which idea?
choices: Universal gravitation and the laws of motion
questions.correctText: Universal gravitation and the laws of motion

questions.prompt: Francis Bacon is associated with the development of which scientific method?
choices: Empirical induction from observation and experiment
questions.correctText: Empirical induction from observation and experiment

questions.prompt: René Descartes' approach to knowledge emphasized which method?
questions.correctText: Systematic doubt and rational deduction

questions.prompt: John Locke's 'Two Treatises of Government' (1689) most directly argued which of the following?
choices: Kings rule by divine right unrestrained by law
Government must rest on consent and protect natural rights
questions.correctText: Government must rest on consent and protect natural rights

questions.prompt: Voltaire's writings most consistently championed which value?
choices: Freedom of expression and religious toleration
questions.correctText: Freedom of expression and religious toleration

questions.prompt: Montesquieu's 'Spirit of the Laws' (1748) is best known for advocating which principle?
choices: Separation of powers among branches of government
questions.correctText: Separation of powers among branches of government

questions.prompt: Rousseau's 'Social Contract' (1762) argued that legitimate political authority derives from which source?
choices: The general will of the people
questions.correctText: The general will of the people

questions.prompt: Denis Diderot is most associated with which monumental Enlightenment project?
questions.correctText: The Encyclopédie

questions.prompt: Cesare Beccaria's 'On Crimes and Punishments' (1764) called for which reform?
choices: Abolition of torture and capital punishment
questions.correctText: Abolition of torture and capital punishment

questions.prompt: Mary Wollstonecraft's 1792 'A Vindication of the Rights of Woman' primarily argued which of the following?
choices: Women should withdraw from public life
Women deserve equal education and rights as rational beings
questions.correctText: Women deserve equal education and rights as rational beings

questions.prompt: Adam Smith's 1776 'Wealth of Nations' advanced which key economic principle?
choices: Free markets guided by an 'invisible hand'
questions.correctText: Free markets guided by an 'invisible hand'

questions.prompt: The Enlightenment 'public sphere' emerged most prominently in which venues?
choices: Salons, coffeehouses, and the print press
questions.correctText: Salons, coffeehouses, and the print press

questions.prompt: Louis XVI's calling of the Estates-General in May 1789 was triggered by which primary problem?
choices: A severe financial and fiscal crisis
questions.correctText: A severe financial and fiscal crisis

questions.prompt: The Tennis Court Oath of June 1789 was a pledge by which group?
choices: The Third Estate, declaring itself a National Assembly
questions.correctText: The Third Estate, declaring itself a National Assembly

questions.prompt: The storming of the Bastille on July 14, 1789, became symbolic primarily because it represented which of the following?
choices: A military victory over a foreign army
Popular rejection of royal absolutism and tyranny
questions.correctText: Popular rejection of royal absolutism and tyranny

questions.prompt: The Declaration of the Rights of Man and of the Citizen (August 1789) most clearly reflects ideas from which thinker?
questions.correctText: John Locke and the Enlightenment

questions.prompt: The October Days of 1789 ended with which dramatic outcome?
choices: The royal family being forcibly brought from Versailles to Paris
The dissolution of the National Assembly
The invasion of France by Prussia
questions.correctText: The royal family being forcibly brought from Versailles to Paris

questions.prompt: The Civil Constitution of the Clergy (1790) had which major political effect?
choices: Reconciliation between revolutionaries and the Catholic Church
Deep division between the Revolution and devout Catholics
Restoration of papal supremacy in France
Abolition of all religion in France
questions.correctText: Deep division between the Revolution and devout Catholics

questions.prompt: The royal family's 1791 flight to Varennes resulted in which political consequence?
choices: Restoration of trust between Louis XVI and revolutionaries
Discrediting of the king and rise of republican sentiment
questions.correctText: Discrediting of the king and rise of republican sentiment

questions.prompt: The Reign of Terror (1793-1794), directed by the Committee of Public Safety under Robespierre, employed which instrument as its grim symbol?
questions.correctText: The guillotine

questions.prompt: The Thermidorian Reaction of July 1794 resulted in which outcome?
choices: The execution of Robespierre and end of the Terror
questions.correctText: The execution of Robespierre and end of the Terror

questions.prompt: Napoleon Bonaparte seized power in France through which event in 1799?
questions.correctText: The Coup of 18 Brumaire

questions.prompt: Napoleon's Civil Code of 1804 is best known for doing which of the following?
choices: Codifying equality before the law and protecting property rights, though limiting women's rights
questions.correctText: Codifying equality before the law and protecting property rights, though limiting women's rights

questions.prompt: Napoleon's Continental System aimed primarily to do which of the following?
choices: Cripple Britain economically through a trade blockade
questions.correctText: Cripple Britain economically through a trade blockade

questions.prompt: Napoleon's 1812 invasion of Russia was disastrous primarily because of which factors?
choices: Scorched-earth retreat, harsh winter, and overextended supply lines
questions.correctText: Scorched-earth retreat, harsh winter, and overextended supply lines

questions.prompt: Napoleon's final defeat occurred at which 1815 battle?
questions.correctText: Waterloo

questions.prompt: The Congress of Vienna (1814-1815), led by Klemens von Metternich, aimed primarily at which goal?
choices: Restoring legitimate monarchies and creating a European balance of power
questions.correctText: Restoring legitimate monarchies and creating a European balance of power

questions.prompt: The Industrial Revolution began in Britain in the late 18th century largely because Britain enjoyed which combination of factors?
choices: Cheap colonial labor and absolute monarchy
Abundant coal, capital, colonial markets, and stable institutions
questions.correctText: Abundant coal, capital, colonial markets, and stable institutions

questions.prompt: James Watt's improvements to the steam engine were most consequential for which sectors?
choices: Textile mills, mining, and later railways and shipping
questions.correctText: Textile mills, mining, and later railways and shipping

questions.prompt: The British Chartist movement of the 1830s-40s primarily demanded which of the following?
choices: Universal male suffrage and political reforms in the People's Charter
questions.correctText: Universal male suffrage and political reforms in the People's Charter

questions.prompt: Robert Owen is best categorized as which kind of early-19th-century reformer?
choices: A utopian socialist model-factory builder at New Lanark
questions.correctText: A utopian socialist model-factory builder at New Lanark

questions.prompt: The Second Industrial Revolution (after 1870) was distinguished from the First by its reliance on which technologies?
choices: Steel, oil, electricity, and the internal combustion engine
questions.correctText: Steel, oil, electricity, and the internal combustion engine

questions.prompt: John Stuart Mill's 'On Liberty' is a foundational text of which 19th-century ideology?
questions.correctText: Classical liberalism

questions.prompt: Edmund Burke's 'Reflections on the Revolution in France' (1790) is regarded as a foundational text of which ideology?
questions.correctText: Modern conservatism

questions.prompt: The Romantic movement of the early 19th century is best characterized by which features?
choices: Emphasis on emotion, nature, nationalism, and the sublime
questions.correctText: Emphasis on emotion, nature, nationalism, and the sublime

questions.prompt: Marx and Engels' 1848 'Communist Manifesto' interpreted history primarily as which kind of struggle?
choices: Class struggle between bourgeoisie and proletariat
questions.correctText: Class struggle between bourgeoisie and proletariat

questions.prompt: The 1848 Revolutions, often called the 'Springtime of Peoples,' resulted in which general outcome by 1850?
choices: Conservative restoration and largely failed revolutions
questions.correctText: Conservative restoration and largely failed revolutions

questions.prompt: The Frankfurt Parliament of 1848-49 failed primarily for which reason?
choices: Inability to secure unification under a liberal constitution acceptable to Prussia
questions.correctText: Inability to secure unification under a liberal constitution acceptable to Prussia

questions.prompt: Italian unification was substantially led by which Piedmontese statesman, who guided the process through diplomacy?
questions.correctText: Cavour

questions.prompt: Giuseppe Garibaldi's most famous military contribution to Italian unification was which campaign?
choices: Conquest of the Kingdom of the Two Sicilies with his Red Shirts
questions.correctText: Conquest of the Kingdom of the Two Sicilies with his Red Shirts

questions.prompt: Otto von Bismarck's foreign policy of Realpolitik prioritized which approach?
choices: Pragmatic pursuit of national interest through diplomacy and war
questions.correctText: Pragmatic pursuit of national interest through diplomacy and war

questions.prompt: The German Empire was proclaimed in 1871 under which monarch following Prussia's victory over France?
questions.correctText: Wilhelm I

questions.prompt: Bismarck's Kulturkampf in the 1870s was aimed primarily against which group within Germany?
questions.correctText: The Catholic Church

questions.prompt: The Ausgleich of 1867 created which political arrangement?
questions.correctText: The dual monarchy of Austria-Hungary

questions.prompt: The Berlin Conference of 1884-85 is best known for doing which of the following?
choices: Ending the slave trade in the Atlantic
Setting rules for European colonization and partition of Africa
questions.correctText: Setting rules for European colonization and partition of Africa

questions.prompt: Belgium's King Leopold II is most notorious for which of the following?
choices: Brutal personal rule and atrocities in the Congo Free State
Liberating slaves in the West Indies
questions.correctText: Brutal personal rule and atrocities in the Congo Free State

questions.prompt: The Suez Canal, opened in 1869, was strategically vital to Britain primarily because it did which of the following?
choices: Connected the Atlantic and Pacific oceans
Provided a shortcut to India and Asia
Linked the Baltic to the Black Sea
questions.correctText: Provided a shortcut to India and Asia

questions.prompt: Charles Darwin's 1859 'On the Origin of Species' was controversial primarily because it proposed which idea?
choices: Evolution by natural selection, challenging traditional creation accounts
questions.correctText: Evolution by natural selection, challenging traditional creation accounts

questions.prompt: Sigmund Freud's 1900 'Interpretation of Dreams' is most associated with which intellectual development?
choices: Founding of psychoanalysis and emphasis on the unconscious mind
questions.correctText: Founding of psychoanalysis and emphasis on the unconscious mind

questions.prompt: Albert Einstein's 1905 special theory of relativity revolutionized physics by challenging which long-held assumption?
questions.correctText: Absolute Newtonian time and space

questions.prompt: Pablo Picasso's 1907 'Les Demoiselles d'Avignon' is widely regarded as a pioneering work in which modernist movement?
questions.correctText: Cubism

questions.prompt: The Dreyfus Affair (1894-1906) in France revealed deep tensions involving which issue most prominently?
choices: Anti-Semitism and the role of the military and Church in the Third Republic
questions.correctText: Anti-Semitism and the role of the military and Church in the Third Republic

questions.prompt: Theodor Herzl's 1896 'The Jewish State' is foundational to which movement?
questions.correctText: Zionism

questions.prompt: The acronym 'MAIN' summarizing causes of World War I stands for which combination?
questions.correctText: Militarism, Alliances, Imperialism, Nationalism

questions.prompt: The immediate spark of World War I in July-August 1914 was which event?
choices: Assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand in Sarajevo
questions.correctText: Assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand in Sarajevo

questions.prompt: On the Western Front, fighting during World War I was characterized primarily by which feature?
choices: Trench warfare and massive industrial-age casualties
questions.correctText: Trench warfare and massive industrial-age casualties

questions.prompt: On the home front during World War I, women's roles changed most notably in which way?
choices: Women took on industrial and clerical work to replace mobilized men
Women were drafted into combat units
Women's suffrage was rolled back across Europe
questions.correctText: Women took on industrial and clerical work to replace mobilized men

questions.prompt: Russia withdrew from World War I via which treaty in March 1918?
questions.correctText: Treaty of Brest-Litovsk
